content: Email notification queuing in Next.js without Redis: use a postgres-backed job table with status enum (pending/processing/sent/failed) and SELECT FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED for worker concurrency safety. For Neon serverless (HTTP driver, no persistent connections), a Vercel Cron job is the right scheduler — define in vercel.json cron config, secure with CRON_SECRET env var. For daily digest: cron hits /api/cron/daily-digest route, queries users with email notifications enabled, chunks into batches of 100, calls resend.emails.batch(). Neon HTTP driver works fine for one-off batch queries. PgBoss is a heavier alternative. For small scale (<10K users), a simple cron + status table + resend batch is sufficient without a dedicated queue.
